title: Supporting Efforts
text:
MDA's Advanced Capability Office (AC) will rapidly develop, demonstrate, and transition disruptive missile defense capabilities to deter, degrade, and/or destroy adversaries' threats to our deployed forces and the U.S. Homeland, in support of next generation missile defense. AC is focused on quickly developing disruptive, "game-changing" capabilities that will provide the offset to the Warfighter to defeat the future threat.
AC is pursuing rapid prototypes for disruptive capabilities utilizing short sprints, with incremental capability off-ramps of to the warfighter. AC is structured as a flat, agile organization, with lean-manning, dedicated project acceleration teams, empowered decision authority, and accelerated acquisition timelines. AC has embedded special programs within the mission areas and collaborates across the DoD enterprise to synergize resources and leverage residual missile defense capabilities.
AC actively engages with the Warfighter throughout the development process, capitalizing on the expertise of an expanding network of partners, and promoting efficient capability transition to service partners or programs of record.
AC has an advanced Modeling, Simulation, and Analysis (MS&A) laboratory that provides element/component-level performance assessment up to mission-level analysis at all classifications. The MS&A lab provides objective evaluation of new concepts, identifies vulnerabilities across the kill web, and informs tactics, techniques, and procedures in operational planning. The lab is on a growth path federate the models across the DOD and OUSD(R&E), providing an integrated approach to missile defeat.
AC will execute discrete projects organized by mission area, with strategic thrusts to include the following:

Show Next Generation Command & Control, Battle Manager and Space (NextGen C2BMS) panel - collapsed
